behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert on specific file paths (AbortSignal.cpp, JSAbortAlgorithm.cpp, JSAbortSignalCustom.cpp), specific symbols (JSCallbackDataWeak, visitAbortAlgorithms, m_abortAlgorithms, m_abortAlgorithmsLock, std::exchange, removeFirstMatching), none of which are mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md only describes the symptom and root cause in general terms (reference cycle, AbortSignal not GC'd) without prescribing specific code changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests mandate gold-specific implementation details: exact type name JSCallbackDataWeak (test_jsabortalgorithm_uses_weak_callback_type), specific method name visitAbortAlgorithms, specific member m_abortAlgorithms, use of std::exchange, and removeFirstMatching/removeIf/.erase. Alternative valid fixes (e.g., breaking the cycle differently, using a different weak wrapper, different storage) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests invoke clang++ -E via subprocess to preprocess files and inspect structural patterns, though they don't actually exercise runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each test asserts presence of non-default patterns that wouldn't exist in a stub; they require actual code modifications.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction describes GC of AbortSignal when pipe never completes; tests don't actually verify GC behavior or pipeTo behavior — they only check structural patterns in C++ source.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names, docstrings, and [pr_diff] fail_to_pass comments grouping them.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a C++ code fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: WEBCCORE_INCLUDES has a typo (extra 'C'); minor but present in test file.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md references pipeTo/AbortSignal at a conceptual level without naming specific files or lines.
